_comment: Machine-readable companion to ts-resolution.md. The accuracy_gate report_matches_committed_doc test asserts these numbers equal the live differential over the committed corpus, so the published report cannot silently drift. Regenerate with: cargo test -p strata-index --test accuracy_gate -- --nocapture print_corpus_report
_by_band_comment: Per-confidence-band calibration (§4.1): does an edge in band B resolve correctly at the rate B claims? precision is edge-level (confirmed/(confirmed+denied)) over SCIP-adjudicable edges; null = undefined (no adjudicable edges, never a vacuous 1.0). unadjudicable = sites in the band SCIP cannot ground-truth, excluded from precision. RESOLVED/EXTRACTED carry no heuristic edges (the heuristic never claims compiler/AST grade).
_will_break_comment: §15.6 resolved by measurement (2026-06-12): the default impact 'will break' confidence cutoff = the lowest band whose MEASURED precision crosses the will-break bar. INFERRED measured 1.00, AMBIGUOUS 0.53, so the boundary sits at the INFERRED band floor (0.40 per §4.1). Edges >= 0.40 are labelled 'will break'; below (AMBIGUOUS) are 'may be affected, review'. impact stays recall-biased by default (surfaces everything); this governs the LABEL only.
